name: Federal CM/ECF
courtSystem: U.S. district, bankruptcy, and appellate courts
scopeNote: National CM/ECF baseline. Each district/bankruptcy/appellate court configures its own file-size cap and local rules may add requirements — set your court's cap once and Raio remembers it.
guidanceNote: These checks are guidance only — not legal advice…tell us at support@macrify.me

constraints.label: PDF-only filing format
constraints.authority: PACER CM/ECF FAQs; AOC CM/ECF PDF-Related Functionality

applicability.note: National CM/ECF accepts PDFs; local courts may add requirements.

constraints.label: Court-configured PDF file size cap
constraints.authority: AOC CM/ECF PDF-Related Functionality; PACER file-size FAQ
constraints.note: No national numeric cap exists; each CM/ECF court configures its own maximum PDF size.

applicability.note: Set the filing court's cap as a local court profile.
Appellate CM/ECF may reject editable forms outright.
National CM/ECF accepts PDF/A but does not require it.
OCR and plain scanned documents are both accepted nationally.

pdfa.note: National CM/ECF lists PDF/A among accepted content types but does not prefer or require conversion.

activeContent.note: CM/ECF rejects PDFs containing JavaScript, scripts that launch external applications, audio, or video.

encryption.note: CM/ECF rejects encrypted or password-protected PDFs.

embeddedFiles.note: CM/ECF rejects PDFs containing internal embedded attachments.

metadataScrub.note: The national AOC document is silent on descriptive metadata; local court FAQ boilerplate may recommend scrubbing, but that is not a national baseline rule.

ocr.note: National CM/ECF accepts both OCR'd documents and plain scanned documents; local courts may set image standards.

flattenForms.note: PACER names un-flattened fillable forms as a common upload-error cause; appellate CM/ECF may reject editable forms outright.
